spec.title: Roadmap verification semantics and placeholder validator

humanSummary.text: Initial audits found P1 validator false negatives for broad integration wording and time-estimate wording. The branch repaired those cases and re-audit found no remaining P0/P1 blockers. Remaining heuristic hardening items are recorded as follow-ups.

findings.title: Path-token surface validator contract
findings.description: The validator now rejects the audited broad wording cases, but the exact contract for generic file/path mentions satisfying integration-surface validation should be captured in tests.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit found no P0/P1 blockers and suggested explicit tests for whether path/package/file tokens alone satisfy integration-surface validation.

findings.impact: Future validator tuning could accidentally over-accept or over-reject integration nodes that mention paths without naming composed runtime surfaces.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Tests define whether file path, package name, command, and artifact tokens satisfy integration-surface validation.
Validator diagnostics explain why a path-only integration node is accepted or rejected.

proposedDagNode.title: Spec DAG surface-token validator tests
proposedDagNode.summary: Define and test how roadmap surface validation treats exact file paths, package names, commands, and artifact tokens.
deliverables: Table-driven roadmap validator tests for file path, package name, command, and artifact surface examples
Validator diagnostic wording that identifies the accepted exact surface token or the rejected generic wording
acceptanceCriteria: Path-only and package-only integration examples have explicit expected pass/fail cases.
Integration-surface validation errors tell spec authors what exact surface wording is missing.
auditFocus: Spec DAG surface validator accepting vague path-token wording

findings.title: Time-estimate phrase fixtures are not table-driven
findings.description: The validator now covers the audited time-estimate false negatives, but the phrase cases should be organized as table-driven fixtures to make future regex tuning reviewable.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit passed the exact time-estimate probes and suggested moving phrase cases into a small table-driven fixture.

findings.impact: Ad hoc estimate phrase tests are harder to extend safely as the roadmap wording validator grows.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Time-estimate rejection examples are table-driven in scripts/spec-dag-validator.test.mjs.
The fixture covers numeric durations, compact durations, qualitative size labels, and story-point wording.

proposedDagNode.title: Table-driven time-estimate validator fixtures
proposedDagNode.summary: Move roadmap time-estimate wording tests into table-driven fixtures for maintainable validator tuning.
deliverables: Table-driven invalid time-estimate fixture in scripts/spec-dag-validator.test.mjs
Coverage for duration units, compact duration tokens, qualitative sizes, and story-point wording
acceptanceCriteria: Adding a new disallowed estimate phrase requires appending a fixture case, not duplicating test logic.
The fixture keeps the live roadmap validation green.
auditFocus: Time-estimate phrase regex changes without focused fixture coverage
